architecture_summary: CvT introduces convolutions into Vision Transformers through a hierarchical multi-stage design with Convolutional Token Embedding and Convolutional Projection, aiming to combine CNN inductive biases with Transformer attention while improving performance and efficiency.

novel_contributions: a new architecture, named Convolutional vision Transformer (CvT), that improves Vision Transformer (ViT) in performance and efficiency by introducing convolutions into ViT to yield the best of both designs
a hierarchy of Transformers containing a new convolutional token embedding
a convolutional Transformer block leveraging a convolutional projection
the positional encoding, a crucial component in existing Vision Transformers, can be safely removed in our model

key_claims.claim: CvT improves Vision Transformer performance and efficiency by introducing convolutions into ViT, combining the best of CNNs and Transformers.
CvT achieves state-of-the-art performance over other Vision Transformers and ResNets on ImageNet-1k, with fewer parameters and lower FLOPs.
Pretrained on ImageNet-22k, CvT-W24 obtains a top-1 accuracy of 87.7% on the ImageNet-1k val set.
CvT can safely remove positional encoding without degradation, simplifying the design for higher resolution vision tasks.
CvT-21 obtains a 82.5% ImageNet Top-1 accuracy, which is 0.5% higher than DeiT-B with the reduction of 63% parameters and 60% FLOPs.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.2. Comparison to state of the art

key_claims.claim: CvT-13 achieves a 81.6% ImageNet Top-1 accuracy, outperforming PVT-Small, T2T-ViT-14, and TNT-S.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.2. Comparison to state of the art

key_claims.claim: Removing position embedding of CvT does not degrade performance, and CvT-13 reaches 81.6% ImageNet Top-1 accuracy without positional embeddings.
Using stride 2 for key and value projection reduces FLOPs by 30% with only a 0.3% drop in ImageNet Top-1 accuracy.
